name: expenses
$comment: Phase 6 wire shape: _action carries {name} only. ExpenseTracker's filter and category-select handlers are no-ops (bind already wrote the value to state.filterCategory / state.addCategory) — the fixture mimics those bind writes via stateMutations. The 'delete' action no longer exists on the demo (the migrated handler doesn't expose one), so the delete-tx-3 step from the old fixture is dropped: parity tests the wire the demos actually speak. MODAL-SWAP-TO-SUCCESS coverage (show-add -> add-invalid -> add-success-render): a REAL valid 'add' is deliberately NOT dispatched here because it appends a transaction with a random id + wall-clock createdAt that would diff across backends (the same reason this fixture never added before). Instead we cover the three modal branches deterministically: the FORM branch (show-add), the VALIDATION branch (add-invalid — a real 'add' with amount '0', which adds nothing so it stays deterministic and returns ok:true per gotcha #4), and the SUCCESS-CARD render branch (add-success-render — inject addSuccessMessage then dispatch a no-op re-render). The success HANDLER (that a valid add SETS the message) is covered by the .NET unit tests; parity's job here is that both backends RENDER each branch identically. expectBodyContains is the per-branch coverage tripwire.

steps.$branch: modal FORM branch — the add modal is open showing the entry form
VALIDATION branch (gotcha #4) — amount '0' is invalid; nothing is added (deterministic), response is ok:true with an inline error, modal stays on the form

SUCCESS-CARD render branch — inject the state a valid add produces (addSuccessMessage + adding), then a no-op re-render; both backends must render the success card identically. Deterministic: no real transaction is appended.
steps.$coverageNote: Assert the RENDER-ONLY modal title 'Transaction added' (emitted by buildVm's success branch, never present in state), NOT the injected addSuccessMessage — that string is echoed back in body.state regardless of what rendered, so asserting it would be vacuous. 'Transaction added' proves the success-card branch actually rendered.
